title: excoecaria acuminata
intro: Excoecaria acuminata, also known as the "yellow oleander", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Philippines, Malaysia, and Indonesia. The leaves of the Excoecaria acuminata plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anthraquinone and cardenolide content. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as a natural antiseptic. Excoecaria acuminata is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk remedies, although its use should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity.
